calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-10-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Smoot appeared by zoom for and with Richard Ragan (Petitioner).

The Court noted that there is supplement that is pending in the e-file queue. Mr. Smoot stated that the supplement addresses the issues that were raised in the Probate Examiner's notes, that the Westinghouse shares were listed on schedule E, that the Petitioner has confirmed that they are not assets of the estate and that he submitted a corrected proposed order. Mr. Smoot also stated that they submitted a corrected schedule E which does not list the Westinghouse stocks, and that the assorted furniture and household items were added to the corrected schedule. In addition, Mr. Smoot stated that the Chase Bank summary for the account ending in 7998 filed on July 9, 2021 shows a balance of $503,345.75 as of July 3, 2021, that the closing statement shows that the title company wire transferred $502,196.76 on June 29, 2021 into the chase account for the estates one half interest of the sale proceeds for the property and that the difference of $1,048. 99 represents the funds that were deposited into the account by the Executor in November of 2020 to pay taxes related to the sale of the real property and dividend income. Furthermore, Mr. Smoot stated that the proposed order has been corrected to identify the amounts that were corrected and that it was submitted to the department yesterday. The Court asked Mr. Smoot to e-mail the corrected order to the Probate Examiner and informed him that it may not approve everything, but it may allow it. The Third Amended Petition for First and Final Report of Executor on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Continued To Mar-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Paul Smoot appeared by zoom for Richard Ragan (Personal Representative).

The Court questions why there has been no response to the issues raised in the Examiner's notes. Mr. Smoot stated that he saw the tentative ruling, but he did not see anything specific, that he had a perfect storm of personal and family health issues, and business issues, that he has no excuse and that they have engaged an estate accounting firm. Mr. Smoot also stated that the accounting firm is in the process of doing an updated accounting and addressing all of the issues raised in the November 9th tentative ruling. The Court informed Mr. Smoot of the local rule that requires that the accountings be for no more than a two-year period. The Frist Amended Petition for First and Final Distribution and Accounting, etc. is continued to March 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
